title: The Chamber of Gof Scene
wikitext:
[]: The "Chamber of Gof" Scene             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Thu Oct 05, 2006 2:16 am IMHO, the "Chamber of Gof" scene in "Death and Rebirth" when Ritsuko gives her speech about Rei is played a little more flat than in the original TV broadcast version. On the TV version, she actually sounds much more dramatic and much more believeable--I truly believe that she is going through a mental breakdown. On the movie dub, she sounds like she's giving a lecture--not real dramatic, and kinda boring. Anyone agree?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 05, 2006 2:20 am It's "Guf". FYI, the only thing that scene has to do with the Chamber of Guf is the fact that Ritsuko mentions it. And I don't listen to the dub if I can help it. Might later for the hell of it.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Thu Oct 05, 2006 2:22 am Thanks--my spelling really takes a dive after 1:00AM. ESPECIALLY after a long day at work! (can you please fix it for me? I'm still getting used to the forum! But the next time you take a listen to the english track, compare the two and you'll see what I mean.

[]: Re: The "Chamber of Gof" Scene             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Oct 05, 2006 5:18 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: . On the movie dub, she sounds like she's giving a lecture--not real dramatic, and kinda boring. Anyone agree?

[]: Re: The "Chamber of Gof" Scene             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Oct 05, 2006 10:14 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: ...On the movie dub, she sounds like she's giving a lecture--not real dramatic, and kinda boring...

[]: Re: The "Chamber of Gof" Scene             Quote Post by Faußtin » Thu Oct 05, 2006 11:32 am cat42 wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: ...On the movie dub, she sounds like she's giving a lecture--not real dramatic, and kinda boring...

[]: Re: The "Chamber of Gof" Scene             Quote Post by cat42 » Thu Oct 05, 2006 4:35 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: ...But I just can't decide if Megumi Hayashibara, or the English dub is the better. It is a huge dilemma for me. Once I've listened to the dubbed version, and her voice both gave me the creeps and made me melt. :P

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Oct 05, 2006 4:51 pm THOU SHALT NOT COMPLIMENT THE AWL.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Sun Oct 08, 2006 1:59 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks: Is it just me, or did so and so happen in the dub? Everyone: OMG DON'T WATCH THE DUB Come on.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Oct 08, 2006 2:09 am zeaven wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks: Is it just me, or did so and so happen in the dub? Everyone: OMG DON'T WATCH THE DUB Come on.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 08, 2006 10:58 am zeaven wrote: KhakiBlueThunderSocks: Is it just me, or did so and so happen in the dub? Everyone: OMG DON'T WATCH THE DUB Come on.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Mon Oct 09, 2006 3:26 am Looks like everyone missed my point.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Oct 09, 2006 3:27 am No we got you're true point, we just had to look past your words, and into your soul . It's a scary place there, people should look at it less.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Mon Oct 09, 2006 3:44 am I'm too used to the dub to not watch it. I guess I'll have to watch the sub eventually so elitist fucks won't turn their noses when I try to talk to them about eva. For those not-so-observant, my point was that he asked a question about the dub, and instead of getting a response (or the more contextually appropriate ignoring), everyone crapped all over him for watching the dub in the first place. If you don't watch the dub or didn't know the answer, this thread is not for you; kindly look elsewhere. No matter how much you dislike the dub, it's a valid (most of the time) representation of Evangelion, and you should appreciate that it attracts people who a) are not anime fans or b) dislike reading subtitles.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Oct 09, 2006 4:00 am zeaven wrote: ...everyone crapped all over him for watching the dub in the first place...

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Mon Oct 09, 2006 6:21 pm cat42 wrote: Read his orignal post, it's a comment critisizing Sue Ulu's further failure as an actoress as the series continued onwards.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Oct 09, 2006 6:38 pm Well reguarding the Eva dub, I like the american Gendo (Tristan MacAvery, not John Swasey) better than Fumihiko Tachiki because Tristan's voice matches the animation better in terms of emotion. (Yes, I know that Fumihiko matches the character better in terms of emotion, but it's so weird to see Gendo yelling, yet have his voice so soft). Also, the FLCL dub kicks ass. I can watch the dub just as easily as the sub, so dubbing can work, if there is the right effort put into it. Of course, Kazuya Tsurumaki did help a bit with the English dub...

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Mon Oct 09, 2006 6:43 pm As I believe the mighty Reichu has said before, they both suck. Learn Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Mon Oct 09, 2006 6:51 pm You have to say one thing about the dub: Grant pwns Miyamura at German. Compare: http://youtube.com/watch?v=fZ43xH0JfC0 (English) http://youtube.com/watch?v=DEoV15Dohbc (Japanese)

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Mon Oct 09, 2006 7:01 pm Wow, didn't even try to pronounce a "V" for "W".
